title: Communication operators
description: Apply electrical and electronic theory and related knowledge, usually under the direction of engineering staff, to design, build, repair, adjust, and modify electrical components, circuitry, controls, and machinery for subsequent evaluation and use by engineering staff in making engineering design decisions. Set up, maintain, and dismantle audio and video equipment, such as microphones, sound speakers, connecting wires and cables, sound and mixing boards, video cameras, video monitors and servers, and related electronic equipment for live or recorded events, such as concerts, meetings, conventions, presentations, podcasts, news conferences, and sporting events. Set up, operate, and maintain the electronic equipment used to acquire, edit, and transmit audio and video for radio or television programs. Control and adjust incoming and outgoing broadcast signals to regulate sound volume, signal strength, and signal clarity. Operate satellite, microwave, or other transmitter equipment to broadcast radio or television programs. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Observe signal lights on switchboards, and dial or press buttons to make connections.
Answer incoming calls, greeting callers, providing information, transferring calls or taking messages as necessary.
Relay or route written or verbal messages, and page individuals to inform them of telephone calls using communication equipment.
Operate telephone switchboards and systems to advance and complete connections for local, long distance, and emergency calls.
Record messages, suggesting wording for clarity, and keep records of calls placed and received, including related toll charges.
Listen to customer requests, referring to directories to answer questions and provide telephone information, and update directory information.
Perform clerical duties such as typing, proofreading, sorting mail, and managing calls while maintaining logs of recordings and pertinent data.
Set up, test, adjust recording equipment for sessions and live performances, and tear down equipment after event completion.
Install, adjust, and operate electronic equipment to record, edit, and transmit audio and video for radio and television.
Compress, digitize, duplicate, and store audio and video data while monitoring strength and clarity of signals to maintain quality broadcasts.
Monitor and log transmitter readings and control audio equipment to regulate volume and sound quality during broadcasts, ensuring good signals.
Confer with producers and other crew members to discuss assignments and determine desired effects and camera movements during productions.
Select sources and preview scheduled programs to ensure signals are functioning and prepare for recording by setting up microphones.
Diagnose and resolve media system problems, report equipment issues and manage necessary repairs or maintenance for broadcast equipment.
Use data entry devices to input and store data, maintain records of work performed, and ensure documents are filed and organized effectively.
Conduct training sessions on the use of audio-visual materials and equipment while providing assistance for users with special needs.
Adjust positions and controls of cameras and related equipment for filming, and operate telecommunications systems to notify recipients of calls.
Monitor emergency and alarm systems, handle emergency calls, and ensure compliance with safety regulations in communication operations.
Direct and coordinate activities of assistants and other personnel during production while providing special assistance during emergencies.
Produce electronics drawings or graphics representing industrial control, instrumentation, or telecommunications networks, using CAD software.
Modify electrical prototypes, parts, or systems to correct functional deviations.
Integrate software or hardware components using microprocessor or control architecture.
Procure parts and maintain inventory and documentation.
Research equipment or component needs, sources, prices, delivery times, or operational costs.
Specify or conduct quality control or quality assurance programs or procedures.
Supervise the installation or operation of electronic equipment or systems.
Compile and maintain records about engineering schematics, equipment, or repairs performed.
Educate equipment operators on the proper use of equipment.
Assemble electrical systems or prototypes, using hand tools or measuring instruments.
Identify and resolve equipment malfunctions, working with manufacturers to procure replacement parts.
Modify, maintain, or repair electronic equipment to ensure proper functioning.
Review electrical engineering plans for adherence to design specifications and compliance with electrical codes.
Read blueprints, wiring diagrams, or engineering instructions for assembling electronic units.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or assist workers.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and material usage.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet operational goals.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Recruit and hire staff members.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services.
Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services.
